heading: Repealed. , (3), ,Pub. L. 87–301, § 24(a)(1)75 Stat. 657Sept. 26, 1961
label: 1182a to 1182c Repealed. , (3), ,Pub. L. 87–301, § 24(a)(1)75 Stat. 657Sept. 26, 1961
notes: Section 1182a, , , related to admission of aliens who were either convicted, or who admitted the commission, of a misdemeanor.act Sept. 3, 1954, ch. 1254, § 468 Stat. 1145

Section 1182b, , , , permitted admission of an alien spouse, child or parent excludable for crime involving moral turpitude in cases of hardship, when not contrary to national welfare or security, and with Attorney General’s consent, and under conditions and procedures prescribed by him. See .Pub. L. 85–316, § 571 Stat. 640section 1182(h) of this titleSept. 11, 1957

Section 1182c, , , ; , , , authorized admission of an alien spouse, child, or parent of a United States citizen afflicted with tuberculosis under terms, conditions and controls prescribed by Attorney General. See .Pub. L. 85–316, § 671 Stat. 640Pub. L. 86–253, § 173 Stat. 490section 1182(g) of this titleSept. 11, 1957Sept. 9, 1959
